meta.source: Public X posts & YouTube debate metadata (Grok-verified accounts merged May 14)

nodes.label: Jay Dyer
nodes.desc: Central Orthobros provocateur. Initiated public attacks on Avery and Wood, engaged in mass blocking of critics. Highest centrality in the conflict network.

nodes.label: C2Antiquity
nodes.desc: Vocal Orthodox defender of Jay Dyer. Posted a strong support thread on May 14 that gained 18k+ views, amplifying the Orthobros position.

nodes.label: OrthoCalling
nodes.desc: Active promoter of Dyer and Orthodoxy. Contributed to loyalty signaling within the Orthobros cluster during the post-debate phase.

nodes.label:
Brazilian
Orthodox
nodes.desc: Tradition defender within the Orthobros cluster. Engaged in post-debate support and amplification of the Orthodox position.

nodes.label:
Orthodox
Elijah
nodes.desc: Strong Dyer defender. Attacked Wood's debate performance on May 13, actively countering the Wood side during the debate phase.

nodes.label: ByJimbob
nodes.desc: Orthobros-aligned content creator. Ran multiple reaction Spaces during the debate phase, including 'Andrew Wilson Puts Wood In The Chipper' — high engagement output.

nodes.label: Epkon406
nodes.desc: Dyer supporter active during the debate phase. Signaled loyalty and engaged in thread defense of the Orthobros position.

nodes.label: David Wood
nodes.desc: Evangelical anti-Islam apologist. Announced a '10 Problems with Eastern Orthodoxy' series on May 14, escalating the conflict to structured content production.

nodes.label:
Andrew
Wilson
nodes.desc: Debated David Wood live on May 13 in 'Why Are OrthoBros Helping Jihadis?' Served as the Orthobros-adjacent proxy in the formal debate phase.

nodes.label:
GodLogic
Avery
nodes.desc: Catalyst for the conflict. Previously collaborated with Dyer on anti-Islam streams, then was attacked by Dyer over grift accusations. Now aligned with Wood side — the flip that triggered the broader schism.

nodes.label: Jai_NDoC
nodes.desc: Active David Wood supporter. Engaged in defending Wood and countering Orthobros narratives during the post-debate phase.

nodes.label:
Rogue
Centurion
nodes.desc: Balanced commentator who offered measured takes during the conflict. Noted dynamics on both sides without strongly aligning.

nodes.label:
Laymans
Seminary
nodes.desc: Posted a detailed logical analysis of the Wilson–Wood debate. Provided substantive commentary rather than tribal signaling.

nodes.label: KurtArthur12
nodes.desc: Observer who noted the irony of both sides having previously collaborated on anti-Islam content, now at war with each other.

nodes.label: notabotIam
nodes.desc: Critic of the drama on both sides. Expressed fatigue with Christian infighting and questioned whether anyone benefits.

nodes.label:
Bandwidth
IsTooSlow
nodes.desc: Questioned the value of debating Jay Dyer at all. Represents the 'why bother' segment of the audience.

nodes.label: Cinemanarchism
nodes.desc: Active neutral commenter. Pushed back on both Dyer and Wood during the post-debate phase, questioning both sides' conduct.

nodes.label:
ICXC
Zachariah
nodes.desc: Consistent neutral engager. Active in debate-phase threads questioning Dyer's conduct without aligning with either faction.

nodes.label:
GaryColeman
5640
nodes.desc: Observer active in blocking discussion threads. Commented on Dyer's pattern of mass-blocking critics during the post-debate phase.

nodes.label: VikingPrime
nodes.desc: Active replier in debate-phase threads around Wood's posts. Engaged without strongly committing to either side.

nodes.label:
General
Audience
nodes.desc: Represents broader audience fatigue voices. Aggregate of viewers, subscribers, and casual commentators expressing exhaustion with the conflict.
